value.prior_session_context: June 25 followed Wednesday's mixed close (S&P 500 7,358.22, Nasdaq 25,476.64) and was dominated by two scheduled catalysts: the 8:30 a.m. EDT release of the May PCE inflation report and the market's reaction to Micron's after-the-bell fiscal Q3 results from June 24. The S&P 500 finished essentially unchanged and the Nasdaq Composite fell for a fourth consecutive session.

text: Thursday, June 25, 2026 was a regular trading day for US cash-equity and futures markets, with no market holidays. The session was framed by the 8:30 a.m. EDT release of the May Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index and by the equity reaction to Micron Technology's fiscal third-quarter results, reported after the close on June 24. US stocks drifted to a mixed close: the S&P 500 ended essentially flat, the Dow rose, and the Nasdaq Composite fell for a fourth straight day.

sp_500.basis: AP: down 0.73 points (less than 0.1%); TheStreet close update -0.01%; previous close 7,358.22

dow.basis: AP: up 71.72 points (+0.1%); TheStreet close update +0.14%; previous close 51,848.90

nasdaq_composite.basis: AP: down 118.03 points (-0.5%); TheStreet close update -0.46%; previous close 25,476.64

russell_2000.basis: AP: up 21.23 points (+0.7%) to 3,007.86 from 2,986.63. AP is the sole authoritative close source for the Russell 2000; TheStreet's intraday +0.38% box figure is inconsistent with the +0.71% implied by AP's point change and is not used.

calculation.method: AP: S&P 500 at 7,357.49 down 0.73 points from 7,358.22; Dow at 51,920.62 up 71.72 points from 51,848.90; Nasdaq at 25,358.60 down 118.03 points from 25,476.64; Russell 2000 at 3,007.86 up 21.23 points from 2,986.63. TheStreet's 4:01 p.m. EDT close update independently lists S&P 500 7,357.49 (-0.01%), Nasdaq 25,358.60 (-0.46%), and Dow 51,920.62 (+0.14%).

text: US equity indexes closed mixed on Thursday, June 25, 2026. The S&P 500 slipped 0.73 points (less than 0.1%, TheStreet -0.01%) to 7,357.49, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 71.72 points (+0.1%) to 51,920.62, the Nasdaq Composite fell 118.03 points (-0.5%) to 25,358.60 in its fourth straight decline, and the Russell 2000 rose 21.23 points (+0.7%) to 3,007.86. Week-to-date, the S&P 500 is down 1.9%, the Dow is up 0.7%, the Nasdaq is down 4.4%, and the Russell 2000 is up 0.9%. Year-to-date, the S&P 500 is up 7.5%, the Dow is up 8.0%, the Nasdaq is up 9.1%, and the Russell 2000 is up 21.2%.

headline.note: Highest annual headline rate since April 2023 (TheStreet); up from 3.8% YoY in April 2026

core_ex_food_energy.note: Core highest since October 2023 (TheStreet); up from 3.3% YoY in April 2026

vs_consensus.core: both monthly and annual in line with forecasts

market_session: Pre-market macro release, 8:30 a.m. EDT Thursday 2026-06-25
text: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, the May PCE price index, was released by the Bureau of Economic Analysis at 8:30 a.m. EDT. The headline index rose 0.4% on the month and 4.1% from a year earlier — the highest annual rate since April 2023 and well above the Fed's 2% target. Core PCE, excluding food and energy, rose 0.3% on the month and 3.4% year-over-year, the highest since October 2023. The annual headline matched the Dow Jones consensus while the monthly figure came in 0.1 percentage point below expectations; both core readings were in line. Personal income rose 0.7%, real PCE rose 0.3%, and the personal saving rate was 3.0%. The data was read as inflation behaving 'pretty much as economists expected,' which let Treasury yields ease.

value.revenue_basis: LSEG consensus; revenue more than quadrupled from $9.3B a year earlier
value.gross_margin_context: up from 74.9% the prior quarter and 39% a year earlier; Investopedia: 'nearly 85% from under 40%'
value.stock_move_basis: Investopedia close section 'jumped 15%'; CNBC headline 'jumps 15%' (extended trading after June 24 print); TheStreet midday +15.78%. Intraday quote widget showed +17.31% and is excluded.
value.trailing_context: Micron stock up roughly 700% over the past year; market cap past $1 trillion

market_session: US regular cash-equity close, Thursday 2026-06-25 (reaction to 2026-06-24 after-bell results)
text: Micron Technology was the single biggest positive driver. Its fiscal Q3 results, reported after the bell on June 24, beat sharply: revenue of $41.46 billion (vs $35.84B LSEG consensus) more than quadrupled from $9.3 billion a year earlier, adjusted EPS was $25.11 (vs $20.78), and gross margin jumped to 84.9% from 39% a year ago on a memory shortage tied to AI data-center demand. Micron guided fiscal Q4 revenue to about $50 billion (vs $43.58B expected) and declared a 15-cent July dividend. The stock rose about 15% on the session, reviving a memory and chip rally that had lost steam earlier in the week. Micron's stock is up roughly 700% over the past year, carrying its market cap past $1 trillion.

apple.driver: Cut on news Apple hiked prices on Macs and iPads/MacBooks; Tim Cook had told the WSJ that 'unsustainable' memory costs could make price hikes 'unavoidable'; Investopedia notes the drop nearly wiped out Apple's year-to-date gain (trading near $277)

microsoft.driver: Announced Xbox console price increases amid the memory crunch (512GB +$100 effective Aug. 1)

nvidia.basis: Investopedia 3:38 p.m. EDT: gave up early gains to trade 2% lower (intraday, mutable)

value.theme: Magnificent 7 / mega-cap tech retreat outweighed the Micron-led memory rally and capped the S&P 500 and Nasdaq

text: Mega-cap technology was the dominant drag and the reason the S&P 500 and Nasdaq could not hold gains. Apple fell about 6% (TheStreet close -6.13%) after it raised prices on Macs and iPads, days after CEO Tim Cook warned the Wall Street Journal that 'unsustainable' memory costs could make price hikes 'unavoidable'; the slide nearly erased Apple's year-to-date gain. Microsoft fell 3.23% after announcing Xbox console price increases tied to the same memory crunch. Nvidia gave up early gains to trade about 2% lower and Broadcom slipped about 1% on Investopedia's late-afternoon read (intraday, mutable). The Magnificent 7's retreat outweighed the Micron-led memory rally.

value.interpretation: Micron's blowout results 'breathed life into a memory and chip stock rally that lost steam earlier this week,' lifting peer memory names even as broad mega-cap tech fell

market_session: US regular cash-equity session (intraday late-afternoon), Thursday 2026-06-25
text: Micron's results revived the memory and chip-stock rally that had stalled earlier in the week. Micron rose about 15%, fellow memory maker Sandisk was up about 22%, and Western Digital gained about 5% on Investopedia's late-afternoon read. These peer moves are intraday-stamped (3:38 p.m. EDT) and mutable; they are carried at medium confidence to distinguish them from the AP-anchored index closes. The rally was sector-specific: memory names rose on Micron's demand signal while the broader large-cap technology complex fell.

ten_year.note: Investopedia: 10-year dropped to 4.40% from 4.42% seen before Thursday's inflation data. TheStreet: fell to 4.394% by the US market close, its lowest point since mid-May.

value.driver: Yields eased after the May PCE report showed inflation behaving roughly as economists expected

text: Treasury yields eased after the in-line PCE report. The 10-year Treasury yield slipped to about 4.40% from 4.42% just before the inflation data (Investopedia), and TheStreet put it at 4.394% by the US market close — its lowest level since mid-May. AP attributed the move to a report showing inflation behaving 'pretty much as economists expected.'

wti_crude.basis: Investopedia 3:38 p.m. EDT: rose more than 2% to $72 a barrel (intraday, mutable; no settlement figure sourced)

brent_crude.basis: Investopedia 3:38 p.m. EDT: up a similar amount at $75.50 a barrel (intraday, mutable)

value.catalyst: Reports that Iran (IRGC) struck a cargo/Singaporean vessel transiting the Strait of Hormuz amid an apparent standstill in US-Iran negotiations; a reversal of the prior several sessions' oil decline on peace hopes
value.context: Strait traffic had picked up since the US and Iran agreed to cease hostilities

text: Oil prices reversed higher after several sessions of declines. West Texas Intermediate rose more than 2% to about $72 a barrel and Brent crude gained a similar amount to about $75.50, on reports that Iran's Revolutionary Guard struck a cargo vessel transiting the Strait of Hormuz amid an apparent standstill in US-Iran negotiations. These are intraday-stamped levels (Investopedia 3:38 p.m. EDT, TheStreet 2:11 p.m. EDT) with no official settlement figure sourced for this cycle; they are mutable and carried at medium confidence with explicit as_of.

dow_leaders.sectors: strength in health care, financials, and industrials offset mega-cap tech losses and lifted the price-weighted Dow

small_caps.note: AP-anchored close; small caps had the strongest showing, closing just above 3,000

value.interpretation: Like Wednesday, the session bifurcated: the cap-weighted, tech-heavy indexes were dragged by mega-cap tech while the Dow and small caps gained on broader participation

text: Beneath the flat headline indexes, breadth was positive. The price-weighted Dow was lifted by Caterpillar (+5.81%) and UnitedHealth Group (+2.65%) and by strength in health care, financials, and industrials, which offset the mega-cap tech losses. Small caps did best: the Russell 2000 rose 0.7% to 3,007.86 (AP close), closing just above 3,000. The pattern echoed Wednesday's bifurcation, with the cap-weighted, tech-heavy indexes held back by a handful of mega-cap names while the rest of the market advanced.

gold.basis: Investopedia 3:38 p.m. EDT: gold futures rose ~1% to $4,045 after dipping below $4,000 on June 24 for the first time since November (intraday, mutable)

bitcoin.note: Near its lowest level this year; earlier fell to ~$58,000 (lows last seen in 2024), off more than 50% from an October peak of $126,000 (intraday, mutable)

fed_commentary.speaker: Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee
fed_commentary.quote: core inflation 'too high' and 'trending the wrong way'
fed_commentary.policy_context: A majority of Fed leaders favor raising rates later this year; the Fed signaled a willingness to hike last week

market_session: US session intraday and post-close commentary, Thursday 2026-06-25
text: Across other assets, gold futures rose about 1% to $4,045 an ounce, recovering after dipping below $4,000 on June 24 for the first time since November. Bitcoin traded around $59,500, near its lowest level this year after briefly touching ~$58,000 (lows last seen in 2024) and off more than 50% from its October peak of $126,000. The US dollar index eased 0.2% to 101.45, and the Cboe Volatility Index rose about 3% to above 19. On policy, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee called core inflation 'too high' and 'trending the wrong way,' consistent with a majority of Fed officials favoring a rate hike later this year. Gold, Bitcoin, and dollar levels are intraday-stamped (3:38 p.m. EDT) and mutable; this claim is carried at medium confidence.
